entries.value: Carbon’s text inputs and text areas replicate the default HTML component operation. Each input field is a tab stop
Avoid using placeholder text whenever possible. Make sure any critical information is communicated either in the field label or using helper text below the field.
Never disable a submit button, even if all of the required fields aren't filled in. Instead, describe what needs to be done using validation and error messages.
Don’t use placeholder text - Putting instructions for how to complete an input, requirements, or any other essential information into placeholder text is not accessible.
Label text is used to inform users as to what information is requested for a text field. Every text field should have a label.and always visible

The type of text field used should reflect the length of the content you expect the user to enter. The default text input is for short, one-line content, whereas text area is for longer, multi-line entries.
Placeholder text: Avoid placeholders when possible. Never use placeholder text to communicate critical information
Place labels directly above the input, and align to the left.
Required field label: Mark required fields with an asterisk (*). If all fields in the form are mandatory, this isn't necessary.
Every text field should have a label. A field without a label is ambiguous and not accessible.
Field labels should be in sentence case.
Text inputs enable users to enter free-form text data. You can use them for long and short-form entries.
Text fields allow users to input custom text entries with a keyboard. Various options can be shown with the field to communicate the input requirements.
Text fields let users enter text into a UI
A text field is an input that allows a user to write or edit text.
When using multiple types of text fields in a UI, separate them by region.
Label text should always be visible
A user needs to input unique information that cannot be predicted with a preset of options.
A user needs to input memorable data that can be entered more quickly in a free-hand format versus a more complex control.
In a single form, mark only the required fields or only the optional fields
When using multiple types of text fields, don't use them next to each other, or within the same form.
Label text shouldn't be truncated. Keep it short, clear, and fully visible.
Label text shouldn’t take up multiple lines.

If a user can only enter an option from a predefined list then avoid using a free-form text input as it is likely to result in an error. Consider using a selection control such as a dropdown, select, or radio button group instead.
label should be no more than 3 words
